name: ExtensionMeasureReport_Group_Population_Subjects
title: R5: What individual(s) in the population (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MeasureReport.group.population.subjects` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MeasureReport.group.population.subjects` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MeasureReport.group.population.subjects` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Group)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MeasureReport.group.population.subjects` has a context of MeasureReport.group.population based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MeasureReport`.
Element `MeasureReport.group.population.subjects` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: What individual(s) in the population (new)
element.comment:
Element `MeasureReport.group.population.subjects` has a context of MeasureReport.group.population based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MeasureReport`.
Element `MeasureReport.group.population.subjects` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Note that this element can only be used for summary and subject-list reports where the basis is of the same type as the populations (e.g. a Patient-based measure, rather than an Encounter-based measure). In addition, the referenced Group resource SHALL be an actual (as opposed to a definitional) group.

element.definition: Optional Group identifying the individuals that make up the population.
element.comment: Note that this element can only be used for summary and subject-list reports where the basis is of the same type as the populations (e.g. a Patient-based measure, rather than an Encounter-based measure). In addition, the referenced Group resource SHALL be an actual (as opposed to a definitional) group.
